title: Market Brief — Wednesday, June 24, 2026
summary: GPU supply is loosening across every class except H100. Util-avail stands at 42% for A100, 39% for H200, 36% for B200, but just 29% for H100, the tightest tier and the only one approaching scarcity. B200 spot surged 14.3% day-over-day to $5.00/hr even as utilization dropped 11.2pp over 30 days, signaling speculative repricing, not organic demand. H100 utilization climbed 5pp to 71.3%, compressing available supply further.
paragraphs: GPU supply is loosening across every class except H100. Util-avail stands at 42% for A100, 39% for H200, 36% for B200, but just 29% for H100, the tightest tier and the only one approaching scarcity. B200 spot surged 14.3% day-over-day to $5.00/hr even as utilization dropped 11.2pp over 30 days, signaling speculative repricing, not organic demand. H100 utilization climbed 5pp to 71.3%, compressing available supply further.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), B200 inference runs $4.58/M tokens for Llama 70B, making it the only GPU where medium-tier pricing breaks even. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output is selling at a loss of roughly $4.24/M against H100 TCO of $7.80/M. OpenAI's gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M output is bleeding even harder. Grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Strong, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 spot at $5.00/hr is overvalued given 36% util-avail slack and falling utilization, a mean-reversion short. H100 at $2.13/hr with only 29% util-avail and rising demand is undervalued, accumulate H100 capacity and sell inference at premium tier for 86% margins.
bullets: 29% util-avail on H100 makes it the tightest compute class, down from 34% implied by last month's utilization.
42% util-avail on A100 signals comfortable supply despite a 13pp utilization drop over 30 days.
$5.00/hr B200 spot is 14.3% above yesterday, the largest single-day GPU price move in today's data.
21.37 on the LLMflation index means frontier token prices have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$0.18/M output from OpenAI gpt-oss-120b undercuts even DeepSeek V3.2 by 47%.
109.8% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest GPU to hold on a cost basis.
